data.title: Cash Dubai crude surges past $170 per barrel, setting historic record
data.description:
Cash Dubai crude prices shattered records earlier on Thursday, breaking above $170 per barrel for the first time in history.

“Cash Dubai crude (balance of the month) just broke above $170 per barrel,” commodity analyst Rory Johnston said on X during midday trading (New York time). “To my knowledge, no crude has ever commanded more than $170/bbl before.”

The unprecedented price spike signals extreme tightness in oil markets amid the war in Iran. While Johnston noted the supply constraints remain localized for now, he warned the situation could deteriorate further.

“Extreme tightness still local, but it will spread with time,” Johnston said in a follow-up post.

The record-breaking prices across Middle East crude benchmarks come in the wake of mounting concerns over global energy supplies and could have far-reaching consequences for consumers and businesses already grappling with elevated fuel costs.

Oil ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL).

Natural Gas ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), and (UNL).

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC).

data.title: Brad Garlinghouse sees Clarity Act odds at 90%: XRP, BTC, ETH make-or-break?
data.description:
In a recent interview on Fox Business, Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse said there’s a 90% chance the Clarity Act passes by end-April, reported by the analyst Crypto Tice—if it passes, will Bitcoin (BTC-USD), Ethereum (ETH-USD), and XRP (XRP-USD) break out, or will a delay slow the momentum?

Ripple CEO's statement just strengthened what Alex Thorn said earlier. He warned that if the crypto market structure bill isn’t approved by the end of April, its chances in 2026 will drop sharply. These statement alignments add strong backing to the bill’s decision timeline.

XRP (XRP-USD) is trading around ~$1.43 after facing rejection near ~$1.60 levels. As per the chart structure, RSI is near ~37, showing weak momentum due to geopolitical tensions going around. Any positive signal in cryptocurrency’s rules and regulations may push XRP to reclaim ~$1.60. However, the current momentum may drag it toward its major $1.35 support area.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is currently in its bearish behavior, trading near ~$69.3K and facing resistance near ~$70K, as per TradingView chart analysis. If the CLARITY Act passes, a break above $70K can push it directly toward ~$75K, but if it fails, a weak trend and low RSI near ~30 may pull the asset back to the ~$64K zone.

Similarly, Ethereum (ETH-USD) is showing a clear downtrend, trading around ~$2.11K after failing to move above the $2.35K resistance level. Its RSI is near ~27, which is close to oversold, so now only a positive signal by the Senate may push it towards the ~$2.50K mark. But no decision or delay combined with current weakness may drag the asset down to its strongest ~$1.80K support zone.

Currently, crypto markets are at a key turning point, with the U.S. crypto regulation bill acting as a major trigger for the next clear trend. The upcoming month could be make-or-break for the industry.

data.title: BYD to invest in R&D center in Brazil as part of Latin America expansion plan
data.description:
BYD Company (BYDDF) (BYDDY) plans to invest about $57M in a new automotive research and testing center in Rio de Janeiro. The project will deepen the company's Brazilian electric vehicle footprint. Construction is expected to start toward the end of 2026, with operations slated to begin around 2028.

The complex will be built on a site of more than 180K square meters near Galeão International Airport, which will give the electric vehicle maker easy logistics access and prominent visibility to international visitors. BYD's (BYDDF) (BYDDY) plan is to create a dedicated hub to test electric vehicles for speed, power output, endurance, durability, and performance under varied driving conditions, including specialized tracks that simulate different road environments and an off-road circuit. Notably, the facility will also generate data specific to tropical climates, helping the company adapt products and technologies to Brazil and other emerging markets.

The center is inspired by a similar BYD testing complex in Zhengzhou, China, and will serve as a key R&D pillar alongside the company’s major manufacturing plant in Camaçari in Bahia. BYD (BYDDY) (BYDDF) executives describe the Rio site as reinforcing Brazil’s strategic role in the company's international expansion and in the future of electric mobility in Latin America.

data.title: Pelthos signals continued ZELSUVMI momentum and targets expanded product launches through 2027
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Pelthos Therapeutics Inc. (PTHS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Scott Plesha, CEO, stated that Pelthos made “great progress” in three key areas: ZELSUVMI’s “substantial demand-generated revenue growth” following its Q3 launch, the acquisition of two FDA-approved products, and “dramatically reduced our quarterly operating and non-GAAP net losses.” He outlined that ZELSUVMI prescriptions increased 129% from the third to the fourth quarter, with 6,232 units prescribed, driving net product revenue from $7.1 million to $9.1 million.
  • Plesha highlighted that “almost all of the revenue in Q4 was generated by units dispensed to customers,” a shift from Q3 when launch stocking contributed significantly to revenue. He also emphasized strong IP protection for ZELSUVMI and the acquisition of Xepi and Xeglyze, calling them “highly complementary FDA-approved products.”
  • Sai Rangarao, Chief Commercial Officer, reported “shipments and prescriptions were ahead of expectations, leading to an increase in internal expectations for 2026.” He cited a rise in unique prescribers to 2,712 and described favorable market access, with ZELSUVMI achieving “59% coverage rate for commercial insurance plans and an incredible 99% coverage rate of Medicaid.”
  • Francis Knuettel, CFO, stated, “net product revenue rose approximately 28% from $7.1 million in the third quarter of 2025, which was our first quarter of launch, to $9.1 million in the fourth quarter of 2025.” He added that cost of goods sold for the quarter was $1.7 million and noted the GTN (gross to nets) for Q4 was 28.7%, “in line with our expectations.”

Outlook

  • Pelthos management has not provided discrete revenue or EPS guidance but indicated confidence in the “revenue growth trajectory” and stated, “we remain extremely confident about our revenue growth trajectory and believe that our current cash balance provides a runway to execute on our business plan.”
  • The company expects to launch Xepi by January 2027 and Xeglyze in late Q2 2027, with the expectation that “ZELSUVMI will remain the main driver of revenue growth,” but the new products are anticipated to add to “revenue growth, margins and profit.”
  • Knuettel indicated that GTNs are expected to move “towards the mid- to high 30% range” going forward due to increased Medicaid business, PBM contracts, and co-pay card utilization.

Financial Results

  • Net product revenue increased to $9.1 million in Q4 from $7.1 million in Q3. Cost of goods sold for Q4 was $1.7 million, reflecting a decrease due to a prior out-of-specification API batch write-off in Q3.
  • Quarterly net operating loss improved to $12.0 million from $15.4 million in Q3, and adjusted EBITDA loss improved to $9.0 million from $11.5 million.
  • Cash balance at December 31 was $18.0 million, up from $14.2 million at the end of Q3. Accounts receivable rose to $8.9 million from $8.0 million. The company closed $18 million in convertible notes in November and drew $30 million in term debt in January 2026.

Q&A

  • Thomas Flaten, Lake Street Capital Markets: Asked about the gross to net discount increase and its drivers. Knuettel responded that the increase is driven by the new PBM contract, higher co-pay card utilization, and greater Medicaid usage.
  • Flaten: Asked about competitive dynamics with Ycanth. Plesha explained that ZELSUVMI’s at-home convenience differentiates it, while Rangarao emphasized positioning ZELSUVMI as “first-line and monotherapeutic.”
  • Brandon Folkes, H.C. Wainwright: Inquired about growth drivers for 2026, asking whether breadth or depth of prescribing would dominate. Plesha noted that “breadth still being perhaps the biggest growth driver,” and Rangarao confirmed the focus on expanding prescriber base.
  • Folkes: Asked about unaided awareness and product positioning with the Xepi launch. Rangarao cited digital campaigns and YouTube advertising and stated ZELSUVMI would remain the first position in the commercial portfolio.
  • David Amsellem, Piper Sandler: Asked about seasonality and product portfolio size. Plesha detailed pronounced seasonal trends in Q2 and Q3 and suggested opportunistic portfolio expansion while maintaining focus on ZELSUVMI.
  • Jeffrey Jones, Oppenheimer: Questioned adoption among pediatricians vs. dermatologists. Rangarao and Plesha noted strong uptake in both, with pushback mainly related to access rather than efficacy or safety.
  • James Molloy, Alliance Global Partners: Inquired about SG&A trends and GTN. Knuettel broke down SG&A components and said SG&A will rise with sales force expansion and product launches, then remain “largely flat throughout the year” except for variable royalty payments. Molloy also asked about ZELSUVMI profitability timing, to which Knuettel responded, “at the end of the year, we will probably cross over that line with what we know now.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed positive to slightly positive sentiment, focusing on growth, adoption, and operational leverage but probing on GTN expansion and SG&A management. Questions were constructive and forward-focused, seeking clarity on drivers rather than expressing skepticism.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently noting “strong value,” “very pleased,” and “extremely confident about our revenue growth trajectory.” They acknowledged challenges but remained optimistic, with Plesha stating, “we are extraordinarily pleased with the receptivity of ZELSUVMI.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management showed increased confidence, with more detailed discussion of operational execution, market access wins, and product expansion, whereas prior commentary was more cautious and focused on early launch validation.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter highlighted a sharp increase in ZELSUVMI prescriptions and net product revenue, with a shift from channel stocking to direct patient dispensing. Management reported improved operating and EBITDA losses and a stronger cash position.
  • Strategic focus has evolved from validating ZELSUVMI’s early launch to scaling market access, broadening the prescriber base, and integrating new product acquisitions.
  • Management’s tone shifted from cautious optimism to strong confidence, while analysts moved from basic launch queries to more strategic questions about profitability timing, commercial leverage, and product lifecycle management.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the expected increase in GTNs as a result of PBM contracts and higher Medicaid usage, which will impact gross margins.
  • SG&A expenses are anticipated to rise temporarily with the sales force expansion and product launch preparations, though management emphasized continued cost discipline.
  • Analysts questioned the timing of profitability and the sustainability of gross to net levels as the business scales.

Final Takeaway

Pelthos Therapeutics reported significant momentum for ZELSUVMI in its first full launch cycle, with prescriptions and unique prescribers rising sharply and a notable shift toward revenue from patient dispensing. The company strengthened its product portfolio with the acquisitions of Xepi and Xeglyze, targeting launches through 2027, and expanded its sales force to support growth. Financial discipline was emphasized, with improved net losses and a stronger cash position, while management expressed confidence in its revenue trajectory and the commercial potential of its portfolio in the coming years.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Trump shows support for continuing Powell investigation
data.description:
President Donald Trump indicated Thursday that a Justice Department probe into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell should continue, despite a key senator maintaining that he will block confirmation of Kevin Warsh as Powell's successor until the investigation is resolved.

“He’s under investigation because he’s building a building for hundreds of billions of dollars more than it’s supposed to cost,” Trump told reporters during a press briefing with the Japanese prime minister, according to CNBC.

The DOJ launched a probe into Powell earlier this year over allegations that he made misleading statements to Congress about the ongoing renovation of the Fed's headquarters in Washington. 

Trump added that Powell “should be lowering rates immediately…but he won’t do that because he’s a stubborn, incompetent person, and that’s a bad thing," according to CNBC.

Trump has nominated Kevin Warsh to succeed Powell as Fed chair. Powell's term is slated to end in May.

data.title: Teck's Fourmile royalty could be worth billions, possibly impacting Barrick's IPO plans - report
data.description:
Teck Resources (TECK) owns a royalty on Barrick Mining's (B) Fourmile gold project that could be worth billions, a revelation that could affect the valuation of Barrick’s planned IPO of its North American business, The Globe and Mail reported Wednesday.

Barrick (B) has not publicly disclosed the Teck (TECK) royalty in a project it has described as "one of the century's greatest gold discoveries."

The only royalty disclosed so far on Fourmile is the 1.6% gross smelter return, which is based on revenue, not profit, held by Royal Gold (RGLD).

According to documents reviewed by The Globe and Mail, if Fourmile is developed into a mine, Barrick (B) would pay Teck (TECK) 10% of the net profits, and the royalty rises to 15% once 6M oz of gold is produced; the royalty agreement was filed in Nevada four years before Barrick (B) made the discovery at Fourmile.

Barrick's (B) planned IPO of its North American mines comprises its 61.5% stake in Nevada Gold Mines, its 60% stake in the Pueblo Viejo mine, both of which are joint ventures with Newmont (NEM), as well as its wholly owned Fourmile project.

With Teck (TECK) set to earn a significant portion of the profits from Fourmile, the value to Barrick (B) is now expected to come in below previous estimates by analysts, potentially lowering the value of the assets to be listed in the IPO, the report said.

data.title: Many individuals have not renewed ACA coverage after subsidy expiration
data.description:
• A new survey found that nearly one in 10 Americans who received health coverage through an Affordable Care Act marketplace plan in 2025 are uninsured in 2026 after enhanced subsidies expired on Dec. 31.
  • The Kaiser Family Foundation found that 9% of those covered through marketplace plans are without coverage this year. 
  • Among those who did renew, 4 out of 5 said that their plan's premiums, deductibles, or coinsurance and co-pays are higher than last year, with about half saying it is "a lot higher."
  • Nearly 3 out of 4 polled said that they are worried about affording costs for emergency care or hospitalizations.
  • Of the 69% of respondents who said they still have marketplace coverage, ~39% have the same plan as last year, while ~28% switched.
  • Of those who switched, 71% said that cost was a major factor, while another 9% said it was a minor reason.

data.title: Freshworks cut as Oppenheimer names stocks to avoid, choose amid agentic AI
data.description:
Oppenheimer downgraded Freshworks (FRSH) to Perform from Outperform and removed its $15 price target while taking a fresh look at its coverage amid the ongoing impact on software names due to AI disruption risk.

The firm noted that its favorite names are Oracle (ORCL), Microsoft (MSFT), and Agilysys (AGYS), and identified Adobe (ADBE), Paycom Software (PAYC), and Freshworks (FRSH) as names to avoid.

Analysts led by Brian Schwartz said that investors will need a finer filter to find the software names that will have a place in the agentic AI future, be durable organic growers, and be positioned to rebound first when sector sentiment improves.

The analysts added that due to a variety of assets in software, it is difficult to paint the group with asingle brush, but broadly they would avoid names that could prove brittle in the face of slowing growth and weakening pricing power.

"Rather, we would focus on names that are on offense (i.e., disruptors), demonstrate stickiness in enterprise IT stacks, and increasing monetization through a tokenization or consumption model. Based on our AI defensibility framework, our favorite names are ORCL, MSFT and AGYS, and we identify ADBE, PAYC, and FRSH (downgrade) as names to avoid," said Schwartz and his team.

The analysts noted that findings in their AI disruption framework point to a period of structural divergence ahead in software. The software names scoring well in their AI defensibility rankings are most likely to sustain or improve growth rates over the medium term, beat estimates, and rebound first, according to the analysts.

Within this framework, the analysts' top picks are Oracle, Microsoft, and Agilysys. "We'vealso identified several poorly positioned software names for the agentic AI future (i.e., ADBE, FRSH, MNDY, PAYC)," the analysts noted.

Schwartz and his team added that the suppliers whose solutions are more function-specific, mainly sell seat-based models, and limit AI revenue disclosures, are most at risk of disruption from nativeAI and faster-moving competitors.

"In our view, the more lightweight, less integrated, and simpler the process being automated, the greater risk of replacement and/or down-selling activity for the software incumbent. We also see greater AI disruption risk among the software names with higher SMB [small and medium-sized business] market exposure. These software names will likely experience a slower recovery in investor sentiment," the analysts added.

Schwartz and his team noted that software company margins should experience strong gains from the cost of coding commoditizing and fewer workers needed to accomplish corporate goals during the agentic era.

"In our findings, most large cap names forecast healthy margin growth over the next two years, and only two SMiD [small and mid] cap names (FRSH and MNDY) show flat-to-down operating margins," the analysts noted.
